rationale: Both give the same technically sound root-cause diagnosis (text-tokenizer EOS folded into all_stop_token_ids overruns the talker's narrow codec vocab) and cite matching file/line evidence for an alleged sanitize_min_tokens_stop_ids guard, but the actual thread only shows the maintainer tagging two others for review ("PTAL") — no confirmed fix or closure. X confidently declares the bug fixed and unilaterally closes the issue, which overshoots what the thread actually established; Y hedges more appropriately with a 'keep-open' disposition, asks for a rebase+retest, and adds a concrete CPU-safe repro script, better matching the escalation-not-resolution pattern in the ground truth. Neither candidate can be fully verified against the sparse ground truth, but Y's calibration is more defensible.
